species: Candidatus Saccharimonadia (uncultured CPR bacterium; ASV-level taxon, TM7 lineage)

growth_records.strain: not isolated (ASV from groundwater 16S rRNA amplicon dataset; dominant in 'Defined' organic-supplement treatments)
growth_records.medium: Oligotrophic groundwater microcosm, 'Defined' treatment (chemically defined organic compounds), no rich media/vitamins/trace elements added
growth_records.cite: Gabashvili et al. Growth of candidate phyla radiation bacteria in groundwater incubations reveals widespread adaptations to oxic conditions. Microbiome (2025). DOI:10.1186/s40168-025-02244-1. [PMC12577380]
growth_records.snippet: The faster-growing members of Cand. Saccharimonadia and Cand. Berkelbacteria only reached doubling times of 6 and 4 days, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (6 days = 144 h) directly reported in text for fastest-growing Saccharimonadia ASVs. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu=ln2/144h=0.004813 h-1. Underlying growth rates themselves were calculated by the authors via linear regression of ln(absolute 16S rRNA gene copy number, from qPCR total counts x relative abundance) vs incubation time; slope = growth rate k (exponential growth model N_t=N_0*e^kt). Organism identity is ASV/candidate-phylum level only (uncultured, mixed community incubation), not a defined axenic strain; incubation temp 15C, dark, rotational shaking, per Methods (general incubation conditions section), medium is oligotrophic native groundwater with treatment-specific organic supplements, exact supplement concentrations not given in main text. [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]
